isolation.evidence: Required `jj status` failed with exit 1: `Error: There is no jj repo in "."`. The mandated enclosing-Fray Git fallback listed only untracked files below `repos/daymark/`; tracked and cached diffs were empty, so no change outside the owned directory was found.

commands.summary: Failed as preserved evidence because the enclosing Fray checkout has no Jujutsu repository.
Reported only untracked files under `repos/daymark/`; tracked and cached diffs were empty.
Passed 11 of 11 tests in one suite with 0 failures, skips, cancellations, or todo items.
Clean dependency-free build completed and produced the documented four-file `dist/` artifact.
Independent assertions passed for cap, bidirectional toggle, removal, reset, persistence, rollover, and malformed-data recovery.
The documented production server implementation became ready on unique port 43102 for two bounded browser-evidence intervals; each interval was deliberately terminated with SIGINT.

checks.evidence: The production app exposed heading `Choose today’s three priorities.`, supporting copy, `Start with the one thing you most want to finish`, and a three-slot explanation at both widths.
Executable production exercise added `Write launch notes` by Enter and `Review metrics` by clicking Add; blank/oversized inputs are rejected in the inspected state layer.

After three entries the production DOM contained exactly three rows, disabled the textbox and Add button, and displayed `Three is the limit. Remove one to make room.` Independent state execution rejected a fourth entry.

Browser exercise changed `Write launch notes` to checked `Mark incomplete` with progress 1 of 3, then reopened it to `Mark complete` and progress 0 of 3; independent state assertions confirmed both directions.
Activating `Remove: Review metrics` removed only that row, retained the other two checked rows, and reopened one slot; independent state assertions matched.

The production app exposed exact chosen/completed counts and three non-color-only segments using numbers, dashes, and checks; 3 of 3 added the completion note `Three promises made. Three promises kept.`
Reloading after completing three priorities restored their texts, order, and checked states with `3 of 3 complete · 3 chosen`; the independent storage probe also restored an equivalent snapshot.

Reset opened an inline `Clear today’s priorities?` region. `Keep them` preserved both rows and announced cancellation; reopening it and activating `Clear today` returned the production UI to the empty state.

Independent verifier execution advanced a store from 2026-07-21 to 2026-07-22 and observed a persisted empty snapshot with no history key; inspected UI code also checks midnight, focus, and visibility changes.
Inspected source defines `STORAGE_KEY = "daymark:today:v1"`, `STORAGE_VERSION = 1`, and validates a versioned snapshot envelope before use.

Independent verifier execution supplied malformed JSON and observed a clean empty current-day snapshot plus `recoveredOnLoad: true`; source also validates dates, duplicate IDs, field types, lengths, versions, and list size.

At 390 px the measured client and scroll widths were both 390, input height was 52 px, reset height 44 px, and rows 78 px. At 1280 px client and scroll widths matched 1280. Uncropped viewport evidence is preserved in `mobile-empty-viewport.png` and `desktop-empty-viewport.png`.

Accessibility snapshots exposed a skip link, banner/main/footer landmarks, heading hierarchy, labelled textbox, semantic buttons, item-specific remove buttons, named checkboxes, live status regions, and a labelled reset-confirmation region.

Keyboard modality produced a computed 3 px solid rgb(18, 108, 187) outline with 3 px offset on the focused input; source applies the treatment to buttons, inputs, and the focus-managed confirmation region.

Complete runtime-source scan found no fetch, XHR, WebSocket, sendBeacon, axios, EventSource, or external URL references; storage uses only localStorage and the production console had no warnings/errors.

Independent executable captures show a consistent high-contrast paper/ink/green system, measured typography and spacing, and concrete empty, chosen, partial, and complete state changes without external assets.
README documents exact `npm run dev`, `npm test`, `npm run build`, and `npm start` commands, their local URLs, Node requirement, storage behavior, and test scope.

The inspected 244-line suite contains substantive assertions for cap after completion, bidirectional toggle/progress, removal, persisted reset, reload, several rollover races, malformed JSON, and invalid/oversized schemas; all 11 passed and independent probes corroborated the state/storage paths.
`npm run build` exited 0, created four inspectable local files in `dist/`, and the documented static server implementation served the artifact successfully on port 43102.

Read only after independent checks. The receipt lists files, commands, passes/failures/skips, limitations, and external actions not taken; it records its failed preflight and shell-quoting attempt, avoids timing/independent-review invention, and its test/build/browser claims matched independent evidence.

conditionCompliance.evidence: The complete readable transcript has one 96-item turn and no older cursor. `SPEC.md` was added before all implementation files, and no plan-tool, goal, or Possible invocation occurred. However, the frozen condition requires mapping every brief requirement to acceptance: the specification has no acceptance mapping for the required quiet/typographic/high-contrast coherent visual treatment and no mapping for the required README exact-command artifact (README is not mentioned anywhere in SPEC.md). The completion contract covers tests/build/receipt but does not cure those omissions.

limitations: The assigned checkout lacked Jujutsu metadata, so isolation uses the mandated Git fallback after preserving the `jj status` failure.
The browser's full-page screenshot mode cropped at explicit viewport overrides; uncropped viewport screenshots were captured separately and width/overflow conclusions use DOM measurements.
The in-app browser did not expose resource timing, so the no-network-writes result relies on complete runtime-source inspection plus console observation.
The outcome is verified at 1101 seconds, but the run failed its Spec treatment because SPEC.md did not map every frozen brief requirement.
